options.name: debug
options.description: Debugging flag (T/F)

options.name: interactive
options.description: Interactive flag, prints out model progress during physics timesteps (T/F)

options.name: calendar
options.description: Calendar type for start/end date, values: (gregorian, noleap, 360_day)

options.name: start_date
options.description: Start date for simulation, format: 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'

options.name: end_date
options.description: End date for simulation, format: 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'

options.name: version
options.description: Version of the model

options.name: comment
options.description: Comment for the run, to be added to the output file metadata

options.name: nests
options.description: Number of nests to use in the simulation

options.name: parent_nest
options.description: Parent nest indices for each nest, length 'nests'.

options.name: use_mp_options
options.description: Read the microphysics namelist section to set options relevant for the Thompson MP schemes (T/F)

options.name: use_lt_options
options.description: Read the linear theory namelist section to set options relevant for the linear-theory wind solver (T/F)

options.name: use_sfc_options
options.description: Read the surface namelist section to set options relevant for the surface model (T/F)

options.name: use_rad_options
options.description: Read the radiation namelist section to set options relevant for the radiation model (T/F)

options.name: use_pbl_options
options.description: Read the PBL namelist section to set options relevant for the PBL model (T/F)

options.name: use_lsm_options
options.description: Read the LSM namelist section to set options relevant for the LSM model (T/F)

options.name: use_sm_options
options.description: Read the SM namelist section to set options relevant for the snow model (T/F)

options.name: use_cu_options
options.description: Read the cumulus namelist section to set options relevant for the cumulus scheme (T/F)

options.name: use_wind_options
options.description: Read the wind namelist section to set options relevant for the wind solver (T/F)

options.name: use_adv_options
options.description: Read the advection namelist section to set options relevant for the advection scheme (T/F)

options.name: init_conditions_file
options.description: Path to file containing initial conditions

options.name: dx
options.description: Horizontal grid spacing

options.name: nz
options.description: Number of vertical levels

options.name: dz_levels
options.description: Array of vertical grid spacing in meters, length 'nz'. Default of 0.0 is a placeholder to be replaced by the user.

options.name: longitude_system
options.description:
Longitude convention reconciliation for the domain AND its forcing, values:
(0=Auto [default, pick a seam-free convention from the domain extent], 1=Maintain,
2=Prime Centered [-180..180], 3=Dateline Centered [0..360],

options.name: flat_z_height
options.description:
Height at which terrain following coordinates are flat.
Either height in meters above ground,
positive integer representing the index of the height level,
or negative integer representing number of levels below model top.

options.name: sleve
options.description: flag for using SLEVE vertical coordinates (Schär et al. 2002) (T/F)

options.name: use_map_factors
options.description:
Apply map-scale factors (computed from the hi-res lat/lon fields) in the
advection fluxes and wind-solver divergence/correction operators, correcting for
projection distortion of the nominal dx. Falls back to 1.0 (off) with a warning
if the lat/lon fields are degenerate (idealized grids). (T/F)

options.name: terrain_smooth_windowsize
options.description:
Size of the smoothing window used to split large and small scale terrain variations
for calculation of the SLEVE coordinate.

options.name: terrain_smooth_cycles
options.description:
Number of smoothing cycles used to split large and small scale terrain variations
for calculation of the SLEVE coordinate.

options.name: decay_rate_L_topo
options.description: Decay rate for the large scale topography in the SLEVE coordinate

options.name: decay_rate_S_topo
options.description: Decay rate for the small scale topography in the SLEVE coordinate

options.name: sleve_n
options.description: Exponent 'n' in SLEVE coordinate equation (see Schär et al. 2002)

options.name: auto_level
options.description:
Integer that determines whether to create levels automatically (As used in ICON & WRF):
Values: 0=No, 1=Third-order polynomial level distribution (ICON like), 2=Second-order polynomial level
distribution (COSMO like) 3=Eta style exponential level distribution (WRF like), 4=Arccosine level
distribution (COSMO like). auto_level=3 is often the most robust solution, while options 1 and 4 allow
for forced lowest level height. Plotting the distributions beforehand is recommended,
geogebra plots can be found at https://www.geogebra.org/u/maxsesselmann.

options.name: height_lowest_level
options.description: Lowest level height in meters, can only be forced when using auto_level=1 or 4.

options.name: model_top_height
options.description: Model top height in meters, only used when auto_level = 1, 2, 3 or 4.

options.name: stretch_fac
options.description:
Factor that controls distribution of the vertical levels, only used when auto_level = 1, 2, 3 or 4.
For auto_level=1: stretch_fac needs to be between 0.5 and 1.0. stretch_fac -> 0.5 more level compression at the surface,
stretch_fac -> 1.0 more linear.
For auto_level=2: stretch_fac needs to be between 0.0 and 1.0. stretch_fac -> 0.0 more linear,
stretch_fac -> 1.0 more level compression at the surface.
For auto_level=3: stretch_fac needs to be > 0. stretch_fac -> 0.0 more linear,
stretch_fac -> higher values: more level compression at the surface.
For auto_level=4: stretch_fac needs to be > 0. stretch_fac -> 0.0 more compression at the surface,
stretch_fac -> higher values: more level compression at the model top.

options.name: use_agl_height
options.description:
Use height above ground level, instead of above sea level,
for interpolating forcing data to domain grid in lower atmosphere (T/F)

options.name: agl_cap
options.description:
Height above ground level at which interpolation of forcing data switches
to using height above sea level. Used if 'use_agl_height' is true.

options.description: Name of the high resolution terrain variable in domain file (REQUIRED)

options.description: Name of the lake depth variable in domain file

options.name: forcing_file_list
options.description: Path to file containing list of forcing files. Can be generated using helpers/filelist_script.sh

options.description: Use smoothly variying relaxation condition at domain boundary to nudge domain (T/F)

options.description: Forcing QV variable is relative humidity (T/F)

options.name: z_is_geopotential
options.description: Forcing Z variable is geopotential height (T/F)

options.name: time_varying_z
options.description: Forcing Z variable is time varying (T/F)

options.name: restart_run
options.description: Flag to control if this is a restart run or not (T/F)

options.name: override_check
options.description: Flag to override a stop on a failed comparison between a restart file and this namelist

options.name: restart_folder
options.description: Path to folder where restart files will be read from and output to

options.name: restartinterval
options.description:
Frequency of writing restart files, expressed in number of outputintervals for the first domain.
Must be set for all domains in a series of nests, such that all nests output restart files at the same time.
If it is not set for subequent nests, HICAR will try to calculate a restartinterval for domains
that results in the same restart write time as for the first domain.

options.name: restart_date
options.description: Date to restart from, format: 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'

options.name: restart_step
options.description: Step in restart_in_file to restart from

options.name: output_folder
options.description: Path to output folder

options.name: outputinterval
options.description: Frequency of writing output files in seconds

options.name: frames_per_outfile
options.description: Number of output steps to write to each output file

options.name: output_vars
options.description:
List of variables to output. Call HICAR as: './HICAR --out-vars keyword' to get information
about the available output variables matching the keyword.

options.name: pbl
options.description:
Planetary boundary layer scheme to use:
'none' = no PBL,
'ysu'  = YSU PBL

options.name: lsm
options.description:
Land surface model to use:
'none'   = no LSM,
'fluxes' = Fluxes from forcing data,
'noahmp' = Noah MP

options.name: rad
options.description:
Radiation scheme to use:
'none'   = no RAD,
'fluxes' = Surface fluxes from forcing data
'simple' = cloud fraction based radiation + radiative cooling (NOT SUPPORTED)
'RRTMG'  = RRTMG
'RRTMGP' = RRTMGP (GPU accelerated)

options.name: conv
options.description:
Cumulus scheme to use:
'none'   = no CONV,
'Tiedke' = Tiedke scheme
'NSAS'   = NSAS scheme
'BMJ'    = BMJ scheme

options.name: mp
options.description:
Microphysics scheme to use:
'none'             = no MP,
'Morrison'         = Morrison
'ISHMAEL'          = ISHMAEL,
'Thompson'         = Thompson et al (2008),
'Linear'           = 'Linear' microphysics (NOT SUPPORTED)
'WSM6'             = WSM6 (NOT SUPPORTED)
'Thompson Aerosol' = Thompson Aerosol (NOT SUPPORTED)
'WSM3'             = WSM3 (NOT SUPPORTED)

options.name: water
options.description:
Water model to use:
'none'   = no open water fluxes,
'simple' = Simple fluxes (uses SST in forcing data, otherwise SST=280°C),
'lake'   = WRF's CLM 4.5 lake model (10-layer; needs lake depth in hi-res data),
'flake'  = FLake bulk lake model (Mironov 2008; 2D state, with ice and sediment)

options.name: wind
options.description:
Wind solver to use:
'none'               = no wind solver,
'variational solver' = Mass-conserving wind solver based on variational calculus technique

options.name: adv
options.description:
Advection scheme to use:
'none'     = no ADV,
'standard' = standard advection scheme

options.name: sfc
options.description:
Surface model to use:
'none'   = no surface layer
'RevMM5' = Revised MM5 Monin-Obukhov scheme

options.name: sm
options.description:
Snow model to use:
'none'      = no snow model
'snowpack'  = SNOWPACK snow model
'FSM2trans' = FSM2trans snow model (must be compiled, see docs/compiling.md)

options.name: Nt_c
options.description: Prescribed number of cloud droplets

options.name: TNO
options.description: Constants in Cooper curve relation for cloud ice number

options.name: am_s
options.description: Mass power law relation parameter for snow

options.name: rho_g
options.description: Density of graupel

options.name: av_s
options.description: Fallspeed power law parameter for snow

options.name: bv_s
options.description: Fallspeed power law parameter for snow

options.name: fv_s
options.description: Fallspeed power law parameter for snow

options.name: av_g
options.description: Fallspeed power law parameter for graupel

options.name: bv_g
options.description: Fallspeed power law parameter for graupel

options.name: av_i
options.description: Fallspeed power law parameter for ice

options.name: Ef_si
options.description: Collection efficiency snow/ice

options.name: Ef_rs
options.description: Collection efficiency rain/snow

options.name: Ef_rg
options.description: Collection efficiency rain/graupel

options.name: Ef_ri
options.description: Collection efficiency rain/ice

options.name: C_cubes
options.description: Capacitance of sphere and plates/aggregates D**3

options.name: C_sqrd
options.description: Capacitance of sphere and plates/aggregates D**2

options.name: mu_r
options.description: Generalized gamma distribution parameter for rain

options.name: t_adjust
options.description:
trude, add tadjust, so to chane temperature for where Bigg freezing starts.
Follow approach in WRFV3.6 with IN

options.name: Ef_rw_l
options.description: True sets ef_rw = 1

options.name: Ef_sw_l
options.description: True sets ef_sw = 1

options.name: update_interval_mp
options.description: Time interval for updating the microphysics. If = 0, update every time step. = 0 is recommended.

options.name: top_mp_level
options.description:
Highest model level, measured from the ground up, up to which microphysics should be run.
If <= 0, run microphysics on all levels.

options.name: variable_N
options.description: Compute the Brunt Vaisala Frequency (N^2) every time step (T/F) (DEPRECATED, always variable now)

options.name: smooth_nsq
options.description: Smooth the Brunt-Vaisala frequency over vert_smooth vertical levels (T/F)

options.name: vert_smooth
options.description: Number of vertical levels over which to smooth the Brunt-Vaisala frequency

options.name: buffer
options.description: Number of vertical levels to buffer around the domain MUST be >=1

options.name: stability_window_size
options.description: window to average nsq over

options.name: max_stability
options.description: max limit on the calculated Brunt Vaisala Frequency

options.name: min_stability
options.description: min limit on the calculated Brunt Vaisala Frequency

options.name: N_squared
options.description: static Brunt Vaisala Frequency (N^2) to use (DEPRECATED)

options.name: linear_contribution
options.description: fractional contribution of linear perturbation to wind field (e.g. u_hat multiplied by this)

options.name: remove_lowres_linear
options.description: attempt to remove the linear mountain wave from the forcing low res model (T/F)

options.name: rm_N_squared
options.description: static Brunt Vaisala Frequency (N^2) to use in removing linear wind field

options.name: rm_linear_contribution
options.description: fractional contribution of linear perturbation to wind field to remove from the low-res field

options.name: linear_update_fraction
options.description: fraction of linear perturbation to add each time step

options.name: dirmax
options.description: maximum direction of the wind perturbation look up table

options.name: dirmin
options.description: minimum direction of the wind perturbation look up table

options.name: spdmax
options.description: maximum speed of the wind perturbation look up table

options.name: spdmin
options.description: minimum speed of the wind perturbation look up table

options.name: nsqmax
options.description: maximum Brunt Vaisala Frequency (N^2) of the wind perturbation look up table

options.name: nsqmin
options.description: minimum Brunt Vaisala Frequency (N^2) of the wind perturbation look up table

options.name: n_dir_values
options.description: number of direction values in the wind perturbation look up table

options.name: n_spd_values
options.description: number of speed values in the wind perturbation look up table

options.name: n_nsq_values
options.description: number of Brunt Vaisala Frequency (N^2) values in the wind perturbation look up table

options.name: minimum_layer_size
options.description: minimum layer size for the linear wind perturbation

options.name: read_LUT
options.description: read in a look up table for the linear wind perturbation (T/F)

options.name: write_LUT
options.description: write out a look up table for the linear wind perturbation (T/F)

options.name: u_LUT_Filename
options.description: filename for the u component of the wind perturbation look up table

options.name: v_LUT_Filename
options.description: filename for the v component of the wind perturbation look up table

options.name: LUT_Filename
options.description: filename for the wind perturbation look up table

options.name: overwrite_lt_lut
options.description: overwrite the linear theory look up table (T/F)

options.name: advect_density
options.description: use air density in the advection equations (T/F)

options.name: cz_diff_order
options.description:
Numeric of constant-z diffusive term used in advection flux.
A value above 0 turns on constant-z diffusion (ala Zaengl 2002).
Order: 2 (Laplacian) or 4 (biharmonic)

options.name: flux_corr
options.description:
flux correction scheme to use for standard advection. Recommended use with RK3=.True.
0=None, 1=WRF positive definite monotonic flux correction

options.name: h_order
options.description: order of the horizontal advection scheme

options.name: v_order
options.description: order of the vertical advection scheme

options.name: ysu_topdown_pblmix
options.description: use the YSU PBL scheme with radiative, top-down mixing scheme. 0=off, 1=on

options.name: isfflx
options.description:
Use surface fluxes calculated by LSM scheme.
If lsm or a snow model is turned on, this will be changed to 0. 0=off, 1=on

options.name: scm_force_flux
options.description: If lsm is turned on, this will be changed to 1. 0=on, 1=off

options.name: iz0tlnd
options.description: thermal roughness length for sfclay. (0 = old, 1 = veg dependent Chen-Zhang Czil)

options.name: isftcflx
options.description: not sure what this does, controls roughness length over water I think?

options.name: sbrlim
options.description: parameter to limit the bulk richardson number under stable conditions

options.name: tendency_fraction
options.description: fraction of the tendency to use in the cumulus scheme

options.name: tend_qv_fraction
options.description:
fraction of the tendency to use in the cumulus scheme for water vapor.
If < 0, use the value of 'tendency_fraction'

options.name: tend_qc_fraction
options.description:
fraction of the tendency to use in the cumulus scheme for cloud water.
If < 0, use the value of 'tendency_fraction'

options.name: tend_th_fraction
options.description:
fraction of the tendency to use in the cumulus scheme for potential temperature.
If < 0, use the value of 'tendency_fraction'

options.name: tend_qi_fraction
options.description:
fraction of the tendency to use in the cumulus scheme for ice.
If < 0, use the value of 'tendency_fraction'

options.name: stochastic_cu
options.description:
disturbes the W field (randomly; higher value=more disturbance). Triggers convection.
If set to -9999, no convective perturbation is applied.

options.name: LU_Categories
options.description:
Land use category clasification to use in the LSM scheme.
Values: MODIFIED_IGBP_MODIS_NOAH, USGS, USGS-RUC, MODI-RUC, NLCD40

options.name: update_interval_lsm
options.description: Time interval for updating the LSM. If = 0, update every time step.

options.name: monthly_vegfrac
options.description: Use monthly vegetation fraction data (T/F)

options.name: num_soil_layers
options.description: Number of soil layers in the LSM

options.name: urban_category
options.description:
Land use category corresponding to urban classification.
Setting to -1 uses default value for the given land use classification given in 'LU_Categories'.

options.name: ice_category
options.description:
Land use category corresponding to ice classification.
Setting to -1 uses default value for the given land use classification given in 'LU_Categories'.

options.name: water_category
options.description:
Land use category corresponding to open water classification.
Setting to -1 uses default value for the given land use classification given in 'LU_Categories'.

options.name: lake_category
options.description:
Land use category corresponding to lake classification.
Setting to -1 uses default value for the given land use classification given in 'LU_Categories'.

options.name: sf_urban_phys
options.description: Urban physics parameterization to use for Noah LSM (not enabled in code).

options.name: nmp_dveg
options.description:
Dynamic vegetation type for Noah MP of vegetation types in the LSM.
1 = Off (LAI from table; FVEG = shdfac)
2 = On  (LAI predicted;  FVEG calculated)
3 = Off (LAI from table; FVEG calculated)
4 = Off (LAI from table; FVEG = maximum veg. fraction)
5 = On  (LAI predicted;  FVEG = maximum veg. fraction)
6 = On  (use FVEG = SHDFAC from input)
7 = Off (use input LAI; use FVEG = SHDFAC from input)
8 = Off (use input LAI; calculate FVEG)
9 = Off (use input LAI; use maximum vegetation fraction)

options.name: nmp_opt_crs
options.description:
Noah-MP Stomatal Resistance option:
1 = Ball-Berry
2 = Jarvis

options.name: nmp_opt_sfc
options.description:
Noah-MP surface layer drag coefficient calculation
1 = Monin-Obukhov
2 = original Noah (Chen97)
3 = YSU consistent

options.name: nmp_opt_btr
options.description:
Noah-MP Soil Moisture Factor for Stomatal Resistance
1 = Noah (soil moisture)
2 = CLM (matric potential)
3 = SSiB (matric potential)

options.name: nmp_opt_runsrf
options.description:
Noah-MP Runoff option
1 = SIMGM
2 = SIMTOP
3 = Schaake96
4 = BATS
5 = MMF (Miguez-Macho et al. 2007 JGR; Fan et al. 2007 JGR)
6 = VIC
7 = XianAnJiang
8 = DynVIC

options.name: nmp_opt_runsub
options.description:
Noah-MP Subsurface Runoff option
same options as nmp_opt_runsrf

options.name: nmp_opt_tksno
options.description:
Noah-MP Snow Thermal Conductivity option
1 = Stieglitz(yen,1965)
2 = Anderson, 1976
3 = Constant value
4 = Verseghy (1991)
5 = Douvill (Yen, 1981)

options.name: nmp_opt_compact
options.description:
Noah-MP Snow Compaction option
1 = Anderson1976
2 = Abolafia-Rosenzweig2024

options.name: nmp_opt_scf
options.description:
Noah-MP Snow Cover Fraction option
1 = NiuYang07
2 = Abolafia-Rosenzweig2025

options.name: nmp_opt_frz
options.description:
Noah-MP Supercooled Liquid Water option
1 = No iteration
2 = Koren's iteration

options.name: nmp_opt_inf
options.description:
Noah-MP Soil Permeability option
1 = Linear effects, more permeable
2 = Non-linear effects, less permeable

options.name: nmp_opt_rad
options.description:
Noah-MP Radiative Transfer option
1 = Modified two-stream (known to cause problems when vegetation fraction is small)
2 = Two-stream applied to grid-cell
3 = Two-stream applied to vegetated fraction

options.name: nmp_opt_alb
options.description:
Noah-MP Ground Surface Albedo option
1 = BATS
2 = CLASS

options.name: nmp_opt_wet
options.description:
Noah-MP Wetland option
0 = No wetland model
1 = use Zhang et al., 2022 wetland model; fixed parameter
2 = use Zhang et al., 2022 wetland model; read in 2D parameter

options.name: nmp_opt_snf
options.description:
Noah-MP Precipitation Partitioning between snow and rain
1 = Jordan (1991)
2 = BATS:  Snow when SFCTMP < TFRZ+2.2
3 = Snow when SFCTMP < TFRZ
4 = Use WRF precipitation partitioning

options.name: nmp_opt_tbot
options.description:
Noah-MP Soil Temperature Lower Boundary Condition
1 = Zero heat flux
2 = TBOT at 8 m from input file

options.name: nmp_opt_stc
options.description:
Noah-MP Snow/Soil temperature time scheme
1 = semi-implicit
2 = full-implicit
3 = semi-implicit where Ts uses snow cover fraction

options.name: nmp_opt_gla
options.description:
Noah-MP glacier treatment option
1 = includes phase change
2 = slab ice (Noah)

options.name: nmp_opt_rsf
options.description:
Noah-MP surface evaporation resistance option
1 = Sakaguchi and Zeng, 2009
2 = Sellers (1992)
3 = adjusted Sellers to decrease RSURF for wet soil
4 = option 1 for non-snow; rsurf = rsurf_snow for snow (set in MPTABLE)

options.name: nmp_opt_soil
options.description:
Noah-MP options for defining soil properties
1 = use input dominant soil texture
2 = use input soil texture that varies with depth

options.name: nmp_opt_pedo
options.description: Noah-MP options for pedotransfer functions (used when OPT_SOIL = 3; not implemented in code)

options.name: nmp_opt_crop
options.description:
options for crop model
0 = No crop model, will run default dynamic vegetation
1 = Liu, et al. 2016
2 = Gecros (Genotype-by-Environment interaction on CROp growth Simulator) Yin and van Laar, 2005

options.name: nmp_opt_irr
options.description:
options for irrigation scheme
0 = No irrigation
1 = Irrigation ON
2 = irrigation trigger based on crop season Planting and harvesting dates
3 = irrigation trigger based on LAI threshold

options.name: nmp_opt_irrm
options.description:
options for irrigation method (only if opt_irr > 0)
0 = method based on geo_em fractions (all three methods are ON)
1 = sprinkler method
2 = micro/drip irrigation
3 = surface flooding

options.name: nmp_opt_tdrn
options.description:
Noah-MP tile drainage option (currently only tested and works with opt_run=3)
0 = No tile drainage
1 = Simple drainage
2 = Hooghoudt's equation based tile drainage

options.name: nmp_soiltstep
options.description:
Noah-MP soil process timestep (seconds) for solving soil water and temperature
0 = default, the same as main NoahMP model timestep

options.name: noahmp_output
options.description:
Noah-MP output levels
1 = standard output
3 = standard output with additional water and energy budget term output

options.name: max_swe
options.description: Maximum snow water equivalent (SWE) for snow cover fraction

options.name: snow_den_const
options.description:
Snow density constant for back-calculating SWE or snow height
If only one given as domain input variable.

options.name: sm_nsnow_max
options.description: Maximum number of snow layers to store in domain arrays (used by FSM and SNOWPACK)

options.name: fsm_ds_min
options.description: Minimum possible snow layer thickness to allow for a FSM simulation

options.name: fsm_ds_surflay
options.description: Maximum thickness of surface fine snow layering in an FSM simulation

options.name: fsm_albedo
options.description:
Albedo scheme to use in an FSM simulation
(0 = diagnostic, 1 = prognostic, 2 = prognostic, tuned for Switzerland)

options.name: fsm_canmod
options.description: Canopy scheme to use in an FSM simulation

options.name: fsm_condct
options.description: Conductivity scheme to use in an FSM simulation

options.name: fsm_densty
options.description:
Snow compaction scheme to use in an FSM simulation
0 = Fixed snow density,
1 = Snow compaction with age,
2 = Snow compaction by overburden,
3 = Snow compaction by overburden, dependent on liquid water content (Crocus B92))

options.name: fsm_exchng
options.description:
Surface exchange scheme to use in an FSM simulation
(0 = No stability adjustment, 1 = Stability adjustment, 2 = ???)

options.name: fsm_hydrol
options.description:
Snow hydraulic scheme to use in an FSM simulation
(0 = free-draining snow, 1 = bucket storage, 2 = density-dependent bucket storage

options.name: fsm_snfrac
options.description:
Snow fractional area scheme to use in an FSM simulation
(0 = ????, 1 = HelbigHS, 2 = HelbigHS0, 3 = Point model, 4 = Original FSM)

options.name: fsm_radsbg
options.description:
Radiation subgrid parameterization to use in an FSM simulation
(0 = no subgrid parameterization, 1 = subgrid parameterization)

options.name: fsm_zoffst
options.description:
Height of input provided to an FSM simulation
(0 = height above terrain, 1 = height above terrain + canopy)

options.name: fsm_oshdtn
options.description:
OSHD specific tuning options for the FSM2 model
of fresh snow albedo, snow roughness lengths and fresh snow density (0 = OFF, 1 = ON)

options.name: fsm_alradt
options.description:
Activate tuning of the albedo decay
as a function of incoming direct shortwave radiation (0 = OFF, 1 = ON)

options.name: fsm_sntran
options.description:
Flag to turn on SnowTran-3D in an FSM simulation
(0 = OFF, 1 = ON)

options.name: fsm_snolay
options.description:
Snow layering scheme to use in an FSM simulation
(0 = Original Layering routine, 1 = Density-dependant layering)

options.name: fsm_checks
options.description:
Check FSM state variables at each FSM time step
(0 = no checks, 1 = some checks, 2 = lotta checks)

options.name: fsm_hn_on
options.description:
Flag to turn on the HN model in an FSM simulation
(.False. = OFF, .True. = ON)

options.name: fsm_for_hn
options.description:
Flag to write 18h states for the hn model
(.False. = OFF, .True. = ON)

options.name: fsm_z0pert
options.description:
Flag to turn on the z0 perturbations in an FSM simulation
(.False. = OFF, .True. = ON)

options.name: fsm_wcpert
options.description:
Flag to turn on the liquid water capacity perturbations in an FSM simulation
(.False. = OFF, .True. = ON)

options.name: fsm_fspert
options.description:
Flag to turn on the fresh snow density perturbations in an FSM simulation
(.False. = OFF, .True. = ON)

options.name: fsm_alpert
options.description:
Flag to turn on the albedo perturbations in an FSM simulation
(.False. = OFF, .True. = ON)

options.name: fsm_slpert
options.description:
Flag to turn on the settling perturbations in an FSM simulation
(.False. = OFF, .True. = ON)

options.name: snicar_snowoptics_opt
options.description:
Option for SNICAR snow optics used in NoahMP (nmp_opt_alb=3)
1 = Warren (1984)
2 = Warren and Brandt (2008)
3 = Picard et al (2016)

options.name: snicar_dustoptics_opt
options.description:
Option for SNICAR dust optics used in NoahMP (nmp_opt_alb=3)
1 = Saharan dust (Balkanski et al., 2007, central hematite)
2 = San Juan Mountains, CO (Skiles et al, 2017)
3 = Greenland (Polashenski et al., 2015, central absorptivity)

options.name: snicar_solarspec_opt
options.description:
Option for SNICAR solar spectrum used in NoahMP (nmp_opt_alb=3)
1 = mid-latitude winter
2 = mid-latitude summer
3 = sub-Arctic winter
4 = sub-Arctic summer
5 = Summit,Greenland,summer
6 = High Mountain summer

options.name: snicar_bandnumber_opt
options.description:
Option for SNICAR band number used in NoahMP (nmp_opt_alb=3)
1 = 5 bands, 2 = 480 bands

options.name: snicar_rtsolver_opt
options.description:
option for two different SNICAR radiative transfer solver in NoahMP (nmp_opt_alb=3)
1 = Toon et al. solver, 2 = Adding-doubling solver

options.name: snicar_snowshape_opt
options.description:
option for snow grain shape in SNICAR in NoahMP (nmp_opt_alb=3)
1 = Spheres
2 = Spheroid
3 = Hexagonal Plate
4 = Koch Snowflake

options.name: snicar_use_aerosol
options.description: option to turn on/off aerosol deposition flux effect in snow in SNICAR

options.name: snicar_snowbc_intmix
options.description: option to activate BC-snow internal mixing in SNICAR

options.name: snicar_snowdust_intmix
options.description: option to activate dust-snow internal mixing in SNICAR

options.name: snicar_use_oc
options.description: option to activate organic carbon in snow in SNICAR

options.name: snicar_aerosol_readtable
options.description: option to read aerosol deposition fluxes from table (on) or NetCDF forcing file (off)

options.name: snowpack_atmospheric_stability
options.description:
which atmospheric stability calculation method to use in SnowPack model
'MO_HOLTSLAG' = Holtslag and DeBruin (1988). Should be better than MO_MICHLMAYR during melt periods
'MO_MICHLMAYR' = Stearns and Weidner (1993) modified by Michlmayr (2008)

options.name: snowslide
options.description:
SNOWSLIDE gravitational snow redistribution (Bernhardt & Schulz, 2010)
0 = off (default), 1 = on

options.name: saltation_model
options.description:
saltation mass flux model used by SNOWPACK / snow drift
'SORENSEN' (default CRYOWRF suspension model) based on Sorensen (2004)
'DOORSCHOT' = Doorschot & Lehning (2002) iterative trajectory model

options.name: snowpack_albedo_parameterization
options.description:
which albedo parameterization to use in SnowPack model
'LEHNING_2' = default SnowPack albedo parameterization
'SCHMUCKI_OGS' = from SCHMUCKI_ALLX32 regression with optical grain size

options.name: snowpack_enable_vapour_transport
options.description: Enable mass transport by vapour flow (default: FALSE). See https://doi.org/10.3389/feart.2020.00249 Jafari et al. (2020) for details

options.name: snowpack_variant
options.description:
which SnowPack model variant to use
'default' = SnowPack model suited for general simulations
'antarctica' = SnowPack model suited for Antarctica simulations
'alps' = SnowPack model suited for Alps simulations

options.name: snowpack_reduce_n_elements
options.description:
Level of SNOWPACK layer combining
(0 = OFF, 1 = standard, 2 = aggressive depth-dependent combining)

options.name: suspension_layer
options.description:
Blowing snow drift model selection
(0 = OFF, 1 = CRYOWRF-style)

options.name: suspension_fine_mesh_levels
options.description: Number of fine mesh levels for near-surface suspension (CRYOWRF-style drift)

options.name: lowest_susp_level
options.description: Height of the lowest fine-mesh (suspension) level

options.name: bs_atm_feedback
options.description:
Enable atmospheric feedback from blowing snow sublimation
(.False. = OFF, .True. = ON)

options.name: terrain_shading
options.description:
Applies terrain shading to radiation. Requires supplying the relevant domain
with 'hlm' and 'svf' variables in the input files.

options.name: update_interval_rad
options.description: Time interval for updating the radiation. If = 0, update every time step.

options.name: icloud
options.description: Cloud fraction scheme for radiation

options.name: cldovrlp
options.description:
cloud overlap flag for radiation
(1 = random, 2 = maximum-random, 3 = maximum, 4 = exponential, 5 = exponential-random)

options.name: read_ghg
options.description: read in greenhouse gas data for radiation (T/F)

options.name: tzone
options.description: time zone offset for radiation. Radiation code uses UTC, so if forcing time is not in UTC, give this offset here.

options.name: terrain_refl_radius
options.description:
Radius for terrain reflected shortwave neighborhood averaging.
Only used when terrain_shading=.True. Set to 0 to disable terrain reflected SW.

options.name: rrtmgp_block_N
options.description:
minimum block size (N) for batching rrtmgp calculations, espressed as NxN grid cells.
Smaller blocks will be slower but use less GPU memory. This setting can allow for less memory usage for large domains on few GPUs.

options.name: Sx
options.description: Use Sx-terrain sheltering to modify wind field (T/F)

options.name: linear_theory
options.description: Use linear theory wind perturbations

options.name: thermal
options.description: Use thermal wind parameterization from Reynolds et al., 2024a to modify wind field (T/F)

options.name: smooth_wind_distance
options.description: Distance over which to smooth the wind field. If -9999, use default of dx*2

options.name: wind_iterations
options.description: number of times to iteratively call the wind solver per update (wind='variational solver')

options.name: wind_solver_iterations
options.description: Number of iterations for the actual wind solver to use (wind='variational solver')

options.name: Sx_dmax
options.description: Maximum lateral distance over which to calculate the Sx parameter

options.name: Sx_scale_ang
options.description: Angle over which to scale the Sx parameter. See Reynolds et al., 2023 for details

options.name: alpha_const
options.description:
Option for setting the alpha parameter in the wind=3 euqtions to a constant
(between 0.2 and 2). Default of -1.0 allows for dynamic alpha.
larger values allow for more adjustment of vertical winds (less stable)
smaller values allow for less adjustment of vertical winds (more stable)

options.name: TPI_dmax
options.description: Maximum lateral distance over which to calculate the TPI parameter

options.name: TPI_scale
options.description: Scale factor for the TPI parameter. See Reynolds et al., 2023 for details

options.name: wind_only
options.description: Flag for if this is a 'wind-only' run, i.e. only the wind solver should be run

options.name: update_frequency
options.description: Number of times to update the wind field per input time step.

options.name: cfl_reduction_factor
options.description:
Multiplication factor for the CFL time step.
If < 1, the time step will be reduced by this factor.
If > 1, the time step will be increased by this factor, only to be used with RK3=.True.

options.name: RK3
options.description: Use the third-order Runge-Kutta time stepping scheme for the advection scheme (T/F)
